items.title: Hundreds arrested in France after wild Champions League celebrations
items.summary: Thousands of officers tried to quell unrest as crowds of fans - some firing flares - celebrated PSG's victory over Arsenal.

items.title: Japan defence minister denies militarism and criticises China's 'huge arsenal'
items.summary: Shinjiro Koizumi makes some of Tokyo's most pointed remarks yet in the dispute over Japan's military build-up.

items.title: Ebola spread in DR Congo 'deeply alarming', MSF warns
items.summary: The medical charity's comments come as the head of the World Health Organization visits the region worst-hit by the virus outbreak.

items.title: 'No-one feels safe now': Residents of Romanian city hit by drone share fears
items.summary: People have just begun returning to check on their homes in the block that was hit early on Friday morning.

items.title: Colombia votes in presidential election that could redefine relations with US
items.summary: The election is held after months of public recrimination between current left-wing President Gustavo Petro and his US counterpart Donald Trump.

items.title: Italy bans Kanye West and Travis Scott concerts over security concerns
items.summary: Local authorities say the cancellation of other West concerts had played a role in the decision.

items.title: Oscar-winning Star Wars editor Marcia Lucas dies aged 80
items.summary: The film editor and ex-wife of director George Lucas was widely recognised as a pivotal creative force behind the original space trilogy.

items.title: South Africa made to look like fools after World Cup visa issues, says minister
items.summary: According to media reports, the team's departure for Mexico on Sunday has been delayed due to an "administrative bungle".

items.title: Trump attacks artists dropping out of US Freedom 250 concert and mulls appearing himself
items.summary: Performers Young MC, Poison frontman Bret Michaels and country singer Martina McBride raised concerns the event will be political.

items.title: US, UK and Australia to develop underwater drone technology
items.summary: The technology, developed under the Aukus military pact, is aimed at protecting undersea cables and boosting naval defence.

items.title: Four more men freed from flooded Laos cave after 10 days
items.summary: Two of the seven villagers who entered the narrow tunnels in search for gold on 20 May are still missing.

items.title: Spain's Sánchez digs in after eight years as PM as wave of scandals threatens survival
items.summary: Corruption probes of colleagues and relatives have put Spain's premier into a fight for survival.

items.title: Ghana's parliament passes anti-LGBTQ+ bill
items.summary: Same-sex acts are punishable by jail terms under Ghana's new bill targeting those identifying as gay, lesbian or transgender.

items.title: How Putin became master of the image
items.summary: From enigmatic KGB agent to wartime ruler, this is how Putin has repeatedly reinvented his image, and himself.

items.title: Cuba’s blackouts leave high-rise residents with constant uncertainty
items.summary: Will Grant spoke to a 70-year-old widow who says the inability to use her building's elevator during a power outage trapped her and her husband when he needed medical care.

items.title: 'It's like a decaying body': Australian farmers battle mouse plague
items.summary: Farmers report thousands of mice per hectare destroying crops and invading homes.

items.title: Ferrari wanted to take on Chinese EVs with the Luce - then the backlash started
items.summary: The brand's first EV has been heavily criticised, with some saying it has abandoned Ferrari's roots.

items.title: Trump's doctor says he's in excellent health - but are US presidents' health checks a PR exercise?
items.summary: Every president in modern history - including Donald Trump this week - has taken an annual physical exam that is as much about messaging as it is about health.

items.title: 'Gifts' from a lover and 'botched' cocaine raids: Police inquiry grips South Africa
items.summary: South Africa's Madlanga Commission hears about Brazilian butt lift bribe denials and alleged drug heists.

items.title: 'Men written by women': How ice hockey romances like Off Campus became TV's hottest new genre
items.summary: The Amazon Prime adaptation of Elle Kennedy's ice hockey romance book is an early hit with viewers.

items.title: Ukraine using AI drones to strike vital convoys supplying Russian troops
items.summary: BBC Verify has analysed videos of attacks in occupied Ukraine on Russian trucks carrying ammunition, fuel and food.

items.title: 'I will sleep with fear': Romanians shaken after block of flats hit by drone
items.summary: A Russian drone hit a block of flats in Romania on Friday, causing a fire and injuring two people, Romanian officials say.

items.title: How did tattoo artists become legal in South Korea?
items.summary: The BBC is in Seoul, where tattooist from all over the country have gathered to celebrate their art, following decades of underground work.

items.title: Why Fifa is being investigated over World Cup ticket prices
items.summary: New York and New Jersey are looking into the association after fans have reportedly been "misled" over ticket sales and seat locations.

items.title: Can Americans spell the National Spelling Bee’s winning words?
items.summary: Shrey Parikh, a 14-year-old, won the competition this year after correctly spelling 32 words in a 90-second lighting round tiebreaker.

items.title: What does Blue Origin rocket mishap mean for Nasa's Moon mission?
items.summary: Science correspondent Pallab Ghosh explains why the explosion is a setback for space exploration.
